jobs.name: github-bounty-income-30m
jobs.prompt: Run deterministic GitHub bounty income pipeline: Scout + Score + Repo Analysis Gate + Report. This unattended job may clone or fetch candidate repositories into .hermes/bounty-workspace for inspection and reporting. After the user explicitly confirms a TAKE target in the current thread, the follow-up workflow is expected to claim or reserve the bounty when the public rules allow it, create a working branch, make the minimal code change, commit, push, and open a PR/submission when those actions are permitted by the repository and bounty terms. It may add comments to GitHub issues or PRs when explicitly part of the safe reporting, claim, or submission workflow. Use GitHub CLI scouting across Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go with bounty/paid/reward/sponsor/good first issue/help wanted/explicit amount terms. Collect title, url, repository, labels, updatedAt, createdAt, commentsCount, and body when available. Exclude radar/artifact aggregation repos, duplicate claim issues, stale or vague tasks without maintainer authorization, private vulnerability requests, unclear payout or acceptance terms, and unauthorized exploit/scanning activity. TAKE requires clear payout terms, acceptance criteria, active maintainer/project signals, low legal/security risk, plausible implementation path, and likely local verification path. WATCH/SKIP must stay quiet by returning DONT_NOTIFY/wakeAgent=false after writing a local report. TAKE must output repo, issue URL, language, bounty evidence, legitimacy reasoning, smallest viable contribution, likely files, tests, claim/reservation path, branch/commit/push/PR path, validation checklist, and rollback/debug path, and trigger the macOS notification command. Do not run unauthorized scanning, bypass platform rules, disclose private vulnerabilities publicly, fabricate findings, spam comments, or publish payout-sensitive data.
